sanskrit:
महात्मानस्तु मां पार्थ दैवीं प्रकृतिमाश्रिताः।
भजन्त्यनन्यमनसो ज्ञात्वा भूतादिमव्ययम्॥
transliteration: mahātmānas tu māṃ pārtha daivīṃ prakṛtim āśritāḥ / bhajanty ananya-manaso jñātvā bhūtādim avyayam
literal_meaning: Great souls, O Pārtha, having taken refuge in the divine nature, worship Me with undivided mind, knowing Me as the imperishable source of all beings.
contextual_meaning: Describes the psychological profile of the integrated personality (mahātmā) who has shifted from egoic to transpersonal identity, experiencing secure attachment to the archetypal Self (Kṛṣṇa) and consequent freedom from material anxiety.
psychological_state: Secure attachment to the Self; ego boundaries softened; reduced amygdala reactivity; increased coherence between prefrontal and limbic systems; sense of being held by a larger intelligence.
trigger_scenario: A high-performing professional, after burnout, discovers meditation and begins to feel an inner anchor that no longer depends on quarterly targets or social media validation; decisions flow from an inner yes/no rather than external pressure.
anchor_text: Rest your mind in the changeless source and live free from fear.
